_description: Structured database of abstraction failure and success patterns. Add entries as experiments are completed. See project_spec.md §10.4 for schema and §12 for taxonomy framework.

patterns.name: Launch Overhead Dominance
patterns.symptom: Abstraction 30-60% slower than native for kernels with execution time < 50 µs
patterns.root_cause_detail: Extra function call layers not fully inlined; runtime dispatch overhead accumulates when kernel execution time is short
affected_workloads: irregular applications with many small launches
mitigation: Coarsen task granularity to amortize launch cost

evidence.note: Julia small (N=32³) eff=0.70 and medium (N=128³) eff=0.69 are consistent with @cuda launch overhead (~0.2–0.5ms) dominating short kernel times (N=32 kernel time ≈0.014ms, N=128 ≈0.16ms). At large N (N=256³, kernel time ≈2.7ms) Julia large eff=1.376 — but this is a thermal artifact (native large session was hotter than Julia large session). Native CUDA overhead (no JIT, no Julia runtime) shows no corresponding degradation at small N. RAJA small eff=0.94 confirms the penalty is Julia-specific, not kernel-size-specific for all abstractions.

patterns.name: Compiler Backend Unrolling Failure
patterns.symptom: Abstraction 2-4x slower on NVIDIA for nested loops; matches on AMD
patterns.root_cause_detail: NVCC/PTX backend fails to unroll template-instantiated loops correctly; AMD LLVM backend handles the same patterns without regression
mitigation: Use vendor-tuned library (cuBLAS) for the failing kernel
File compiler bug report with minimal reproducer

evidence.note: Benchmark eff=0.24 is thermally contaminated (sequential session: raja_naive runs after ~130s of native+cublas load; 2.76x thermal ratio confirmed by fresh-state nsys profiling at 116 GFLOP/s vs benchmark 42 GFLOP/s). Thermal-corrected eff=0.66 (116/175.1) is the representative value. True root cause is P004 API Limitation (RAJA forall cannot express shared-memory tiling); P002 Compiler Backend Failure not confirmed — cudaLaunchKernel overhead is 0.05% of runtime. Full compiler backend validation (P002) pending ncu hardware counters on A100 (RmProfilingAdminOnly=1 blocks hardware PMU on RTX 5060).

patterns.literature: Godoy et al. 2023 — Julia DGEMM on A100

patterns.name: Memory Layout Mismatch
patterns.symptom: 20-40% bandwidth reduction vs. native; L2 hit rate drops from ~75% to ~45%
patterns.root_cause_detail: Abstraction defaults to non-optimal array layout (e.g., LayoutRight when LayoutLeft is required for NVIDIA coalescing); causes strided instead of unit-stride access
affected_workloads: multi-dimensional arrays with high data reuse
mitigation: Explicitly specify Kokkos::LayoutLeft for NVIDIA kernels
Profile L2 hit rate before and after layout change
Document layout requirements per platform in config.yaml

evidence.note: Kokkos small eff=0.76. RAJA small eff=0.94 with identical kernel logic but direct block/thread mapping — P003 layout mismatch NOT confirmed. Kokkos uses MDRangePolicy<Rank<3>> with tiling hint {2,4,32}; tiling overhead (block scheduling, boundary handling) is more likely than layout mismatch. Kokkos large eff=0.703 is thermally biased (native large session ran in hotter GPU state). P003 remains unconfirmed in E3 — see P006 (Tiling Policy Overhead) as the more likely explanation.

success_patterns.name: Layout-Induced Coalescing Advantage
success_patterns.symptom: Abstraction efficiency > 1.0 vs cuBLAS ceiling reference
success_patterns.root_cause: Column-major Fortran layout in Julia eliminates transposition overhead; warp-level memory access patterns are superior to row-major C implementations at all compute-bound problem sizes
success_patterns.root_cause_detail: Julia column-major CuArrays map warp threads (varying threadIdx.x → row) to consecutive elements of the same matrix column, giving perfect coalescing on A and broadcast access on B. The native tiled C kernel uses row-major storage and incurs 512 __syncthreads() barriers at N=8192 (256 tiles × 2 barriers). At arithmetic intensity ≈ 683 FLOP/byte (compute-bound), synchronisation overhead exceeds shared-memory bandwidth savings. Fresh-state nsys profiling confirms the advantage is size-independent: eff≈1.29 at N=4096 (medium) and eff≈1.20–1.25 at N=8192 (large). The apparent non-monotonic benchmark scaling (0.80 medium → 1.25 large) was a thermal contamination artifact, not a size threshold.

evidence.validation_method: CUDA event timing (CUDA.@elapsed) confirms GPU-only kernel time produces same GFLOP/s as wall-clock measurement; max_rel_err < 1e-6 at N=128. Fresh-state nsys profiling (profiles/e2/julia_naive_medium_nsys.nsys-rep) confirms medium-N advantage: 225 GFLOP/s fresh vs 139 GFLOP/s benchmark (1.62x thermal ratio). Large-N benchmark/profiling delta is only 4%, confirming large-N result is thermally clean.
evidence.kernel_storage_order_confirmed: nsys kernel name contains (Int)1 storage-order parameter, confirming column-major layout

success_patterns.implication: Layout conventions can dominate algorithmic overhead at large problem sizes. Expert optimisation assumptions (tiling always improves performance) do not hold in compute-bound regimes where synchronisation overhead dominates. Decision framework must condition tiling recommendation on measured arithmetic intensity.
success_patterns.mitigation: N/A — this is a performance advantage for the abstraction. For the native kernel: use cuBLAS or profile-guided tiling decisions conditioned on N.
success_patterns.ppc_impact: PPC > 1.0 — abstraction exceeds native baseline; counts as excellent portability but native baseline is not the theoretical ceiling

_notes: All patterns marked 'hypothesis' must be validated with experiment evidence before paper submission.
Add 'evidence' array entries referencing experiment_id values from data/performance.csv.
Change status from 'hypothesis' to 'validated' once experiment_ids are added.

P002 is 'partially_validated': raja_naive/medium benchmark eff=0.24 is thermally contaminated (2.76x thermal ratio; see profiles/e2/e2_profiling_notes.md). Thermal-corrected eff=0.66. True root cause is P004 API Limitation (RAJA forall cannot express tiling), not P002 Compiler Backend Failure. P002 classification pending ncu hardware counters on A100.

P005 is 'validated': julia_naive/large efficiency=1.25 confirmed by GPU event timing and correctness check. Deep profiling (2026-03-15) further confirms Pattern 5 is size-independent: fresh-state eff≈1.29 at N=4096, eff≈1.20 at N=8192. Medium-N benchmark eff=0.80 was a thermal artifact, not a size threshold for the pattern.

Thermal Contamination Pattern (informally P004_Thermal): sequential benchmark sessions create differential thermal loading when kernel durations vary across abstractions. Warmup-50 is insufficient — must use max(50 iterations, 60s) for variable-duration kernels. This affects E2 medium-N results for both raja_naive and julia_naive.

P006 candidate: Tiling Policy Overhead at Small Granularity — Kokkos MDRangePolicy with large tiling hints {2,4,32} shows eff=0.76 at N=32³ vs RAJA eff=0.94. At very small grids, tile boundaries dominate active work cells, and block scheduling overhead amortizes poorly. Validated direction: E3 evidence. Needs ncu occupancy analysis at N=32 to confirm.

E3 hypothesis verdict: memory-bound stencil → all abstractions near-native PARTIALLY confirmed. RAJA at medium eff=0.99 confirms hypothesis. Kokkos medium eff=0.91 supports it. Thermal instability prevents clean verification at N=256 without locked clocks. Julia medium eff=0.69 confirms P001 Launch Overhead Dominance (now validated). P003 Memory Layout Mismatch NOT confirmed in E3 — RAJA vs Kokkos divergence points to tiling overhead (P006), not layout.
